title: [lora_l2.h:87-89] rx_cb_t callback signature exposes internal snr/rssi types
description:
Gap found: API type safety in header not reviewed.

Why this was missed: Header API design patterns not systematically checked.

The actual bug: The lichen_lora_rx_cb_t typedef (lines 87-89) uses int16_t rssi and int8_t snr. These match Zephyr's lora_recv() output types, but:

1. The callback is part of the public API (exported in header)
2. If Zephyr changes lora_recv's rssi/snr types, this typedef would need updating
3. Callers may expect different types (e.g., float for SNR)

This is a minor API stability concern, not a bug. The current types are correct and match Zephyr's convention. The header comment should note that these types match Zephyr's lora API.

Priority 4 - API documentation.
close_reason: Fixed: Added documentation noting rssi/snr types match Zephyr lora_recv() API with update guidance
